So the perfect webinar script again guys is something I could go over for hours, but this is kind of what it looks like Okay, and I'll go through it really really quickly Usually that there's three parts of a webinar.
There's the intro There's the content and then there's the the pitch the close so the intro again We start with the bold promise give them a hook to the end something about If you stand the end this webinar I'm gonna give you this this cool prize or this bonus or whatever we command attention We tell them to put away Skype or Facebook you try to get their attention We qualify ourselves and why you're an expert why they should listen to us We future-pace them at what's gonna happen if they if they consume this webinar and gain from it future-pace them, right?
Then we jump into the content of the webinar most people When they do their webinars, they're trying to again.
They're trying to teach people five or six or seven things For a webinar to work really really well same thing that we talked about all the other scripts you guys You're focused on one thing.
What's the one core message that you need to?
To get your audience to believe to buy what it is you're selling Okay, and then everything else wraps around that so for us we pick one core thing Okay, and usually this one core thing is We tied to headline this is how to blank without blank If you can fit that if you can fit your thing you're selling into that little framework It works, okay?
For example one of our recent webinars was high-ticket secrets It was how to sell high-ticket products without picking up the phone How to do what without what?
Okay, that's the one thing and then I go and I share secrets that There aren't separate secrets or secrets that strengthen the one thing the one core argument Okay, and and each of these secrets is to break and rebuild their pattern So if my if my one thing again is about how to sell high-ticket things without selling on a phone Secret number one is probably gonna be something it's something it's a pattern there It's a belief they have in their mind Okay, they believe right now that to sell high-ticket things they have to be on the phone secret number one Here's how I do it without a phone.
Okay, I'm gonna share them the secret I'm gonna in that process break their belief that they have to have the phone And I'm gonna rebuild it with the truth with the truth that I want them to understand and believe The secret number two is maybe I can know I can do that, but it's gonna take a lot of traffic So secret number two is how to do this with almost no traffic I got to break their belief and then rebuild their belief patterns to what I need them to believe to buy my thing and Secret number three is the same thing Okay, so I'm finding out what their belief patterns are that they're keeping them from buying my thing I show them the pattern I break it and I rebuild it what I need them to believe Okay, that's the content section of your webinar and from there we transition I say let me ask you a question I just showed you some cool stuff But how would you like for me to help you do this and we transition to our clothes and again?
I could spend days on this clothes.
I'm not gonna go through it in a hole, but just to kind of give you Just kind of give you a really quick rundown The first thing we do the the overall clothes Process is what I call a stack I learned this from a guy named Armand Moran The stack has literally made me at least ten million dollars in the last five or six years It's how I do every one of my presentations What it does is basically what I what I'm sharing my offer I share here's component number one and I talk about it and then here's component number two But you notice how I stack them I share component number three and I stack them again number four stack them so if you ever look at my my presentations you'll notice that that in fact It's almost humorous sometimes like it first thing you get is this that means blah blah blah blah So that means the first thing you get is this and then here's second number thing and I keep retelling this by time I got the end you've heard me stack all the the the components of the offer like ten times But what we found is if you don't do it that way People only remember the last bonus or last thing you told them And so they're justifying the price of what you're selling based on the last thing you told them And so you want before you tell them any kind of price You want them to see the entire offer everything they're getting in a huge stack.
That's what we call it the stack Okay, and then we pitch it high we do our if-all statements just like I showed you guys in the last script We do price high drop price low and we ever called action Okay, then in throughout this throughout the stack There's a bunch of different closes and I'm not gonna go through these right now But these are a bunch of different clothes that we use all each I'll drop one or two or three.
Well, I'll probably drop I don't know maybe seven or eight of these different closes throughout this whole process And if you google mostly as you can find them some of these are mine Some of them I learned from from other stage presenters some I learned from a lot of different people If you google some of these you'll find out what these closes are We also have a have a product at a perfect webinar calm that we I go into a lot more detail about this so That's kind of how we do a webinar pitch
